game_name: Ultima IV: Quest of the Avatar

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Establishes player's primary virtue focus; shapes class and initial capabilities
Core mechanical/narrative framework; mastery required for Avatar ending
16x larger map than Ultima III; requires spatial navigation and NPC interaction
Actions raise/lower virtues; feedback from Hawkwind on progress
Required to access shrines; meditation and mantra repetition grants enlightenment
Valor virtue improved by not fleeing; present in dungeons and overworld
Final dungeon; access to Codex of Ultimate Wisdom required for Avatar status
Culmination of all eight virtue enlightenments; game conclusion

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully improving a virtue through actions
Reaching a shrine after acquiring its rune
Progressing toward enlightenment in a virtue
typical_tension_triggers: Actions that lower virtues (bragging, fleeing combat)
Encountering moral dilemmas with no single correct answer
Getting lost in large overworld (16x map size)
Approaching Stygian Abyss without full enlightenment
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Contemplative exploration punctuated by combat and moral choice; shifts from character-building self-reflection to increasingly dangerous dungeon trials

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Shrines scattered throughout Britannia; accessible only when player possesses corresponding rune

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle; emphasis on exploration, NPC interaction, and virtue-building through dialogue choices and alms-giving
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate; shrine location requirements introduce puzzle-solving and combat challenges; virtue enlightenment gates progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalating; Stygian Abyss presents cumulative danger requiring all eight virtues; final dungeon climax
notable_spikes: Stygian Abyss entry (requires complete virtue enlightenment)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each shrine requires player to possess the corresponding virtue's rune before entry. Meditation and correct mantra repetition three times grants enlightenment. Creates progression gate based on character development rather than pure combat power.

NPCs present ethical choices without single correct answers. Player actions (giving alms, fleeing/standing firm, humble/bragging responses) dynamically raise or lower virtues, affecting NPC interactions and overall progress.
Dungeons feature puzzle-filled rooms of increasing complexity. Shift from overworld exploration to environmental puzzle-solving mid-game.
Stygian Abyss serves as single-point gate requiring all eight virtues to be achieved. Represents ultimate test combining all mechanics and progression.
